This test verifies the global exception handling chain catches type-mismatch errors and produces a clean 4xx, not a 5xx leak. NOT 5xx because the test is checking that the framework's binding error is being translated; if it leaks as 500 then either the global handler is missing or some custom handler is wrong.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | 1) Obtain an admin token via `adminToken()`.<br>2) GET `<users>/abc/activity` with `Authorization: Bearer <admin>` — `"abc"` cannot be bound to a Long.<br>3) Assert status is in 400..499 and typically 400.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | • Status 4xx (typically 400 Bad Request)<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Type-mismatch isn't producing a clean 4xx.<br>• NOT 5xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Type-mismatch exception leaked as 500. Indicates a missing `@ControllerAdvice` for `MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException` or a custom handler that's mishandling the case.<br>• NOT 2xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Controller declared the path variable as `String` instead of `Long` and accepted the garbage input — the path variable must be typed correctly.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| 28  | TC28 — S1-F12 size=0 must NOT 5xx (graceful handling)                                              | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST registerPath()` (setup), `POST loginPath()` (setup), `GET <users>/{ownId}/activity?size=0`                                                                                                                      | The spec is silent on `size=0` so the test is intentionally lenient: the only requirement is "do not 5xx". The reason this is non-trivial is that Spring's `PageRequest.of(0, 0)` constructor throws `IllegalArgumentException` because page size must be ≥ 1. A controller that builds a `PageRequest` directly from the query string without validating size will leak that exception as a 500. The fix is to either validate (`@Min(1)`) and return 400, or clamp to a minimum (`Math.max(1, size)`) and return 200. Either is acceptable — what's not acceptable is letting the exception escape.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | 1) Register a fresh user and login to capture token.<br>2) GET `<users>/{ownId}/activity?size=0` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.<br>3) Read the response status and assert it is NOT in 500..599.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | • NOT 5xx (any other code is acceptable)<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** `PageRequest.of(page, 0)` threw `IllegalArgumentException` and leaked as 500 because the controller constructed the page request without validating size. The student must either validate (Bean Validation `@Min(1)`) or clamp the value before constructing PageRequest.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| 29  | TC29 — S1-F12 size=-1 returns 4xx                                                                  | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST registerPath()` (setup), `POST loginPath()` (setup), `GET <users>/{ownId}/activity?size=-1`                                                                                                                     | Negative page size is semantically invalid — there is no meaningful interpretation of "give me -1 results per page". Spring's `PageRequest.of(page, -1)` throws on negative size. The controller must validate the parameter and reject with strictly 4xx (not 5xx). Stricter than TC28: a negative value is unambiguously bad input, where size=0 was a borderline edge case.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | 1) Register a fresh user and login to capture token.<br>2) GET `<users>/{ownId}/activity?size=-1` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.<br>3) Assert status is in 400..499 and not 5xx.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | • Status 4xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Controller doesn't validate the `size` parameter.<br>• NOT 5xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Negative value crashed `PageRequest.of()` and the exception leaked.<br>• NOT 2xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Controller silently accepted the bad value (perhaps clamped to 0 or a default) and returned 200 — but a `-1` is not a "soft" misinput; it should be rejected loudly.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| 30  | TC30 — S1-F12 size=string returns 4xx                                                              | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST registerPath()` (setup), `POST loginPath()` (setup), `GET <users>/{ownId}/activity?size=abc`                                                                                                                    | Spring's `@RequestParam Integer size` binding must reject the string `"abc"` because it cannot be converted to an integer. The framework will throw `MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException` which the global exception handler must translate to 400. The mirror of TC27 but for query parameters instead of path variables. NOT 5xx because type-mismatch on a query param should produce a clean 400, not a server crash.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | 1) Register a fresh user and login to capture token.<br>2) GET `<users>/{ownId}/activity?size=abc` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.<br>3) Assert status is 4xx, typically 400.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | • Status 4xx (typically 400 Bad Request)<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Type-mismatch on the query param is not being translated cleanly.<br>• NOT 5xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** `MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException` leaked as 500 — needs a `@ControllerAdvice` handler.<br>• NOT 2xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Controller declared the param as `String` and tried to parse it manually with no error handling — must use `Integer` typing so the framework catches it.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| 31  | TC31 — S1-F12 cross-user activity (regular user A reads User B) returns strictly 403               | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST registerPath()` × 2 (setup), `POST loginPath()` (setup), `GET <users>/{B.id}/activity` with A's token                                                                                                           | The S1-F12 ownership violation contract is STRICTLY 403, not the 403-or-404 leniency of TC17 (User CRUD). The spec is explicit: when A's token is valid and B exists, the response must be 403 (forbidden), because the resource exists but the caller lacks permission. 404 would imply A doesn't even know B exists, which is a different (and incorrect) policy decision for activity feeds. This stricter contract catches controllers that conflate "not your data" with "not found" — a privacy choice that conflicts with the spec's explicit 403 mandate for this endpoint.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | 1) Register A and capture A's id.<br>2) Register B and capture B's id.<br>3) Login as A and capture A's token.<br>4) GET `<users>/{B.id}/activity` with `Authorization: Bearer <A's token>`.<br>5) Assert status is strictly 403.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | • Status strictly 403<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Anything else is a contract violation.<br>• NOT 5xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Ownership check threw uncaught.<br>• NOT 2xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** A read B's activity feed — privacy breach. Likely cause: ownership not checked at all, or token's `uid` claim isn't being compared to path id.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| 32  | TC32 — S1-F12 admin reads any user's activity returns 2xx                                          | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST registerPath()` (setup customer), `adminToken()` (setup), `GET <users>/{customer.id}/activity` with admin                                                                                                       | The ADMIN role must bypass the strict 403 ownership check from TC31 for the activity feed. Mirrors the User-CRUD admin override (TC21) but for the activity sub-resource. The check confirms the role-based policy is wired uniformly across endpoints: the same ADMIN escape-hatch must work everywhere or it's not really a role, just an inconsistent set of one-off branches. Strict 2xx with content array because admin override is unconditional per spec.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | 1) Register a fresh attendee via `POST registerPath()` and capture their id.<br>2) Obtain an admin token via `adminToken()`.<br>3) GET `<users>/{attendee.id}/activity` with `Authorization: Bearer <admin>`.<br>4) Assert 2xx and parse body to confirm `content` is an array.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | • Status 200..299<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Admin override missing from the activity endpoint specifically — common when the ownership check is hardcoded in the controller method instead of expressed via `@PreAuthorize` that includes the ADMIN bypass.<br>• Body has `content` array<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Wrong response shape — controller returns a different envelope for admin callers vs regular callers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 33  | TC33 — S1-F12 page=-1 returns 4xx                                                                  | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST registerPath()` (setup), `POST loginPath()` (setup), `GET <users>/{ownId}/activity?page=-1`                                                                                                                     | Spring's `PageRequest.of(int page, int size)` requires `page >= 0`. A negative page number throws `IllegalArgumentException` from the constructor. The controller must validate or clamp the page parameter before constructing the PageRequest, returning 4xx for negative values. NOT 5xx because letting the framework's argument-check exception leak is exactly the lazy-handler pattern this test targets.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | 1) Register a fresh user and login to capture token.<br>2) GET `<users>/{ownId}/activity?page=-1` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.<br>3) Assert status is 4xx and not 5xx.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | • Status 4xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Controller doesn't validate page index.<br>• NOT 5xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** `PageRequest.of(-1, ...)` threw and the exception leaked. The controller must validate (`@Min(0)`) or clamp before constructing PageRequest.<br>• NOT 2xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Negative page silently accepted (perhaps by clamping to 0) — but spec demands explicit rejection of nonsensical input.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |

| 50  | TC50 — S4-F10 Ticket analytics scanRate is 0 when no scans exist                                   | `public`, `features_m2`    | `GET /api/tickets/analytics/dashboard`                                                                                                                                                                                | When no Cassandra scan events have been recorded, the `scanRate` in the dashboard must equal 0.0. a booking with `appointmentDate` set 7 days in the future.<br>2) Seed a sale for that booking with `COMPLETED` status.<br>3) PUT `/api/sales/<saleId>/refund` with a reason body.<br>4) Assert 2xx and verify `refundAmount` = full original amount.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | • Status 2xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Refund strategy lookup throws or is not wired.<br>• `refundAmount` = full original amount<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Wrong strategy selected — partial or no refund applied despite the full-refund window being active.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

This is the strictest refund-window boundary test.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | 1) Seed a booking with `appointmentDate` = now + 1 hour (or past).<br>2) Seed a COMPLETED sale for that booking.<br>3) PUT `/api/sales/<saleId>/refund` with a reason.<br>4) Assert 2xx and verify `refundAmount` = 0.0.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | • Status 2xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Wrong strategy applied — no-refund window ignored, full refund given.<br>• `refundAmount` = 0.0<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Strategy pattern not implemented; fixed 100% refund always returned.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| 61  | TC61 — S5-F12 Refund with 24-48h window applies partial refund strategy                            | `public`, `features_m2`    | `PUT /api/sales/<id>/refund`                                                                                                                                                                                          | The `PartialWindowRefundStrategy` (50% refund) must be applied when the booking date is 24-48 hours away. This tests the middle branch of the three-strategy decision tree.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | 1) Seed a booking with `appointmentDate` = now + 36 hours.<br>2) Seed a COMPLETED sale.<br>3) PUT `/api/sales/<saleId>/refund` with a reason.<br>4) Assert 2xx and verify `refundAmount` ≈ 50% of original amount.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | • Status 2xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** 24-48h window not handled; falls through to no-refund or full-refund.<br>• `refundAmount` ≈ 50% of original<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Partial strategy not implemented; 0% or 100% always returned.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |

| 66  | TC66 — S2-F10 Full-text search is case-insensitive                                                 | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST /api/events/<id>/index`, `GET /api/events/search/full-text?q=<UPPER>`                                                                                                                                           | Elasticsearch full-text search must be case-insensitive. `"CHECKED_IN"` in history<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Cassandra SELECT omits the scanType column, or the DTO mapping ignores it.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| 73  | TC73 — S5-F10 Revenue-by-tier GENERAL tier has correct totalRevenue                                | `public`, `features_m2`    | `GET /api/sales/analytics/revenue-by-tier`                                                                                                                                                                            | The GENERAL tier entry in the revenue-by-tier response must sum only COMPLETED sales for GENERAL tier tickets. This catches tier aggregation that includes PENDING or REFUNDED sales. `/api/sales/analytics/revenue-by-tier`.<br>3) Find GENERAL entry and assert `totalRevenue` = 200.0 (±1.0).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | • GENERAL `totalRevenue` = 200.0 ±1.0<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Aggregation includes all statuses, inflating revenue with PENDING amounts.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| 74  | TC74 — S2-F11 Re-indexing event updates stale ES document                                          | `public`, `features_m2`    | `PUT /api/events/<id>` (update name), `POST /api/events/<id>/index`, `GET /api/events/search/full-text?q=<new name>`                                                                                                  | After updating an event's name and re-indexing via the manual index endpoint, the full-text search must return the event for a query matching the new name. This catches implementations that index only on creation, ignoring subsequent updates.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 1) Seed and index an event with name `"oldname<nonce>"`.<br>2) Update the event's name to `"newname<nonce>"`.<br>3) POST `/api/events/<id>/index` to re-index.<br>4) GET `/api/events/search/full-text?q=newname<nonce>`.<br>5) Assert list size ≥ 1.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | • Search returns updated event<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Manual index endpoint does not replace the stale ES document — update-then-re-index pattern fails because the index call inserts a new doc instead of upserting.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

`/api/bookings/analytics/dashboard`.<br>3) Assert `averageAmount` between 100.0 and 200.0.            | • `averageAmount` between 100.0 and 200.0<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Controller returns `totalAmount` sum instead of AVG, or a hardcoded 0.0 placeholder. | 78  | TC78 — S3-F11 Attend CANCELLED booking returns 400                                                 | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST /api/bookings/<id>/attend`                                                                                                                                                                                      | Recording attendance for a CANCELLED booking must return 400. Attendance can only be recorded for bookings that were actually fulfilled (CONFIRMED or COMPLETED status). This catches services that call `neo4jTemplate.save()` regardless of booking status.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | 1) Seed a CANCELLED booking via `_EtM1Seed.seedBooking()` with status CANCELLED.<br>2) POST `/api/bookings/<id>/attend` with admin token.<br>3) Assert status = 400.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | • Status 400<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** No status guard — service writes an ATTENDED edge for a booking the user never showed up for, poisoning the | 79  | TC79 — S2-F10 Search returns multiple matching events                                              | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST /api/events/<id>/index` × 2, `GET /api/events/search/full-text?q=<keyword>`                                                                                                                                     | When multiple events share a keyword, the full-text search must return all of them. The test seeds two events with the same keyword, indexes both, and asserts list size ≥ 2. This catches ES queries that use `term` (exact-match) instead of `match` (full-text), which would return at most one result.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | 1) Seed two events with `"concert<nonce>"` in the name; index both.<br>2) GET `/api/events/search/full-text?q=concert<nonce>`.<br>3) Assert list size ≥ 2.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | • List size ≥ 2<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** ES query uses `term` filter (no tokenization) so only an exact-match returns — second event with same keyword not found.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 80  | TC80 — S4-F11 Scan history entry has timestamp field                                               | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST /api/tickets/<id>/scan`, `GET /api/tickets/<id>/scan-history`                                                                                                                                                   | Each entry in the Cassandra scan history must include a `scannedAt` (or `timestamp`) field showing when the scan occurred. This catches Cassandra schemas that omit the `WRITETIME` or scan-time column.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 1) Scan ticket via `POST /api/tickets/<id>/scan`.<br>2) GET `/api/tickets/<id>/scan-history`.<br>3) Assert first entry has `scannedAt` or `timestamp` non-null.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | • Timestamp field non-null<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Cassandra table schema missing the time column, or the DTO omits it from the `SELECT` projection. | 81  | TC81 — S5-F11 Multiple audit entries for same sale are all preserved                               | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST /api/sales/<id>/audit` (called twice)                                                                                                                                                                           | Calling the audit endpoint twice for the same sale must create two separate MongoDB documents (append-only audit log pattern). The test calls the endpoint twice and verifies the count is 2. This catches implementations that upsert on sale id, overwriting the previous audit entry.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | 1) Call POST `/api/sales/<id>/audit` twice with different reason bodies.<br>2) Query MongoDB via `_EtM2S5.audit(saleId)` and assert count ≥ 2.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | • MongoDB document count ≥ 2<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Service uses `mongoTemplate.upsert()` with a `{saleId}` filter — second call overwrites the first instead of appending a new document.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | • `transactionCount` ≥ 3<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** COUNT includes PENDING/REFUNDED sales, inflating the transaction count beyond the completed-only figure. Only COMPLETED sales can be refunded. Refunding a PENDING sale would confuse the payment state machine.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 1) Seed a PENDING sale.<br>2) PUT `/api/sales/<id>/refund` with a reason and admin token.<br>3) Assert status = 400.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | • Status 400<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Refund strategy selector runs on any status — PENDING sale transitions to REFUNDED without ever being COMPLETED, bypassing the payment cycle.                    | • `ticket_id` in Cassandra row matches seeded ticket id<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Scan writes the booking id or event id as the partition key instead of the ticket id —                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |

                            | • `scanRate` ≈ 0.5<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Dashboard counts CHECKED_IN status from PostgreSQL instead of querying Cassandra scan events — the two sources may • `refundStrategy` key present in JSONB<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Service applies the strategy but does not write the strategy name to the JSONB audit — operators cannot determine post-hoc collect their ids.<br>2) POST `/api/events/index/batch` with `{"ids":[id1,id2,id3]}`.<br>3) Assert 2xx or 404 (if batch endpoint not in spec).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | • Status 2xx or 404 (endpoint may be optional)<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Batch endpoint exists but only processes the first id — subsequent ids silently dropped.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| 114 | TC114 — S4-F12 Scan history pagination works correctly                                             | `public`, `features_m2`    | `GET /api/tickets/<id>/scan-history?page=0&size=1`                                                                                                                                                                    | The scan history endpoint must support pagination. This catches implementations that ignore pagination parameters and always return all rows. Scan ticket twice.<br>2) GET `/api/tickets/<id>/scan-history?page=0&size=1`.<br>3) Assert result count = 1 (not 2).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | • Result count = 1<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Cassandra query ignores `LIMIT` clause — all rows returned regardless of page size | • Attended event E1 not in recommendations<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Cypher query returns all events co-attended by similar users without filtering out already-attended ones — user gets recommended events they've already seen.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |

                                                                                                      | • Status 403<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Index endpoint secured with `authenticated()` | 123 | TC123 — S3-F11 Neo4j ATTENDED edge count monotonically increases                                   | `public`, `features_m2`    | `POST /api/bookings/<id>/attend` (called 3 times with different bookings)                                                                                                                                             | Each successive attend call for the same user-event pair must increment `attendanceCount` by 1. The test calls attend 3 times and reads the edge count after each call, verifying 1 → 2 → 3.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | 1) Call attend 3 times with 3 different bookings for the same user-event pair.<br>2) After each call, query Neo4j.<br>3) Assert counts are 1, 2, 3 respectively.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | • Counts 1, 2, 3 after each attend call<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Neo4j MERGE increments by more than 1 (e.g., runs the increment statement twice per call due to a double-save bug). | 124 | TC124 — S4-F12 Scan history limit parameter respected                                              | `public`, `features_m2`    | `GET /api/tickets/<id>/scan-history?limit=1`                                                                                                                                                                          | When a `limit` parameter is provided, the scan history endpoint must return at most that many results. With 3 scan records and `limit=1`, only 1 result must be returned.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | 1) Scan ticket 3 times.<br>2) GET `/api/tickets/<id>/scan-history?limit=1`.<br>3) Assert result count = 1.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | • Result count = 1<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Cassandra ALLOW FILTERING without LIMIT clause ignores the parameter — all 3 rows returned regardless of `?limit=1`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | 1) POST `/api/sales/<id>/audit`.<br>2) Read the MongoDB document.<br>3) Assert `createdAt` or `timestamp` is non-null.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | • Timestamp non-null<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** MongoDB document inserted without setting a creation time — audit log cannot be sorted or queried by time.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |

The test seeds an event where the keyword only appears in the description (not the name), indexes it, and verifies the search returns it.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | 1) Seed an event with description containing `"acoustic<nonce>"` but name not containing it; index.<br>2) GET `/api/events/search/full-text?q=acoustic<nonce>`.<br>3) Assert list size ≥ 1.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | • List size ≥ 1<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** ES document only indexes the `name` field — description-only matches are never returned.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

                                                           | 1) Seed attendance for user A across multiple events shared with user B.<br>2) GET `/api/bookings/recommendations/<userId>?limit=1`.<br>3)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | • Result count ≤ 1<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Cypher query has no LIMIT clause — all matching events returned regardless of client-specified limit. JDBC set currentUses=5<br>2) GET `/api/sales/discounts/top-used?limit=20`<br>3) Assert 2xx                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | • HTTP 2xx<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Student excludes inactive discounts from the top-used ranking                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

## Design Pattern Test Cases

| #   | Title                                                                      | Tags                                            | Endpoint(s)                                                                                      | What it tests                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Pre-conditions                                                              | Steps
