| 379 | TC379 — DP-1 Strategy: RefundStrategy interface exists                     | `public`, `patterns`, `strategy`                | (reflection — no HTTP)                                                                           | Per Section 3.2, the Strategy pattern requires a `RefundStrategy` interface with exactly one abstract method named `calculateRefund`. This is the contract every concrete strategy must implement and the entry point the selector dispatches to at runtime. Asserting the interface shape via reflection is the cheapest way to detect a malformed Strategy implementation before exercising any HTTP path.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Compiled sales-service classpath available.                                 | 1) Reflection-load `RefundStrategy`.<br>2) Assert it is an interface (`Class.isInterface()`).<br>3) Enumerate abstract methods; assert exactly one whose name equals `calculateRefund`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | • RefundStrategy is an interface<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Strategy modeled as a concrete class or abstract base — defeats polymorphic dispatch.<br>• Exactly one abstract method named calculateRefund<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Extra abstract methods bloat the contract and force every concrete strategy to override irrelevant methods.                                                                                                                |
| 380 | TC380 — DP-1 Strategy: 3 concrete strategies implement RefundStrategy      | `public`, `patterns`, `strategy`                | (reflection — no HTTP)                                                                           | Per Section 3.2, the spec mandates three concrete strategies named exactly `FullWindowRefundStrategy`, `PartialWindowRefundStrategy`, and `NoRefundStrategy` (theme.json `s5StrategyFullRefund`/`s5StrategyFoodOnly`/`s5StrategyNoRefund`). Each must implement `RefundStrategy` so the selector can return any of them through the common interface. The window-based names reflect that dispatch is purely time-based: how far the event is from the refund request moment.                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | TC379 passes.                                                               | 1) Reflection-load each of the three classes by exact name from theme.json.<br>2) For each, assert `RefundStrategy.class.isAssignableFrom(strategyClass)`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | • All 3 concrete strategy classes load by name<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Class missing or named differently — selector cannot return the expected type.<br>• Each implements RefundStrategy<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Class exists but does not implement the interface — won't compile in `selector.select()` return position.                                                                                                                            |
| 381 | TC381 — DP-1 Strategy: RefundStrategySelector exists                       | `public`, `patterns`, `strategy`                | (reflection — no HTTP)                                                                           | Per Section 3.2, the dispatch logic must live in a separate `RefundStrategySelector` (or `RefundStrategyFactory`) class — not in the service. The service should only call `selector.select(sale, hoursUntilEvent).calculateRefund(...)`. A separate selector keeps the service decoupled from the time-window decision.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | None.                                                                       | 1) Reflection-scan sales-service classpath for a class named `RefundStrategySelector` or `RefundStrategyFactory`.<br>2) Assert a `select`-like method exists.<br>3) Assert its return type is `RefundStrategy`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | • Selector class exists<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Selection logic embedded in the service — TC385 catches the consequence; this catches the structural cause.<br>• select() returns RefundStrategy<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Method returns a concrete strategy type — defeats polymorphic substitutability and forces the caller to know which strategy ran.                                                                                               |
| 382 | TC382 — DP-1 Strategy: FullWindowRefundStrategy stored in transactionDetails | `public`, `patterns`, `strategy`               | `POST /api/sales/{id}/refund-window-policy` (event > 48 h from now)                             | Per Section 3.2 step (d), invoking S5-F12 for a TicketSale whose linked Event is more than 48 hours in the future must select `FullWindowRefundStrategy` and write its name into `TicketSale.transactionDetails.refundPolicy` (PG JSONB). The stored class name is the runtime evidence of which branch ran — without it the test cannot distinguish a wrong strategy from the right one when both produce similar numeric outputs. This is an important Event Ticketing difference from other themes: the strategy audit lives in PG JSONB rather than MongoDB.                                                                                                                                           | COMPLETED TicketSale for an Event with eventDate = NOW() + 7 days.         | 1) `_EtM2S5.bookWithEvent(this, 1L, 1L, "COMPLETED", dateFar)` where dateFar=NOW()+7d.<br>2) `_EtM2S5.sale(this, bid, 1L, 100.0, "COMPLETED", dateFar, null)`.<br>3) POST `/api/sales/{sid}/refund-window-policy` with body `{"reason":"customer_canceled"}`.<br>4) Assert 2xx with refundAmount≈100.<br>5) JDBC SELECT `transactionDetails::text` FROM TicketSale WHERE id=sid; parse JSON; assert `refundPolicy` equals `FullWindowRefundStrategy`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | • 2xx with refundAmount≈100 (full refund)<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Strategy returned wrong amount or wrong strategy selected for far-future event.<br>• transactionDetails.refundPolicy == FullWindowRefundStrategy<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Selector picked PartialWindow even though event is > 48h away — silent dispatch bug masked by similar output; or refundPolicy field missing entirely.                                                         |
| 383 | TC383 — DP-1 Strategy: PartialWindowRefundStrategy stored in transactionDetails | `public`, `patterns`, `strategy`          | `POST /api/sales/{id}/refund-window-policy` (24 < hoursUntilEvent ≤ 48)                         | Per Section 3.2 step (e), a TicketSale for an Event 24–48 hours in the future must select `PartialWindowRefundStrategy` and refund exactly 50% of the sale amount. The JSONB field `transactionDetails.refundPolicy` must record the class name. This exercises the middle window branch, which is the branch students most often collapse into either FullWindow or NoRefund due to off-by-one errors on the 48-hour boundary.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | COMPLETED TicketSale for Event with eventDate = NOW() + 2 days (≈ 36–48h). | 1) `_EtM2S5.bookWithEvent(this, 1L, 1L, "COMPLETED", dateMid)` where dateMid=NOW()+2d.<br>2) `_EtM2S5.sale(this, bid, 1L, 200.0, "COMPLETED", dateMid, null)`.<br>3) POST `/api/sales/{sid}/refund-window-policy` with body `{"reason":"schedule_conflict"}`.<br>4) Assert 2xx with refundAmount≈100 (50% of 200).<br>5) JDBC SELECT transactionDetails; parse; assert `refundPolicy` equals `PartialWindowRefundStrategy`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | • 2xx with refundAmount≈100 (50% partial)<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Wrong arithmetic — refunded full amount or zero.<br>• transactionDetails.refundPolicy == PartialWindowRefundStrategy<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Selector ignores the 24-48h window and routes to FullWindow or NoRefund.                                                                                                                                                               |
| 384 | TC384 — DP-1 Strategy: NoRefundStrategy 400 + MongoDB audit               | `public`, `patterns`, `strategy`                | `POST /api/sales/{id}/refund-window-policy` (hoursUntilEvent ≤ 24)                              | Per Section 3.2 step (f), a TicketSale for an Event that is today or already passed (≤ 24 hours until event) must select `NoRefundStrategy`, return 400 with reason "refund window expired", AND emit a `REFUND_DENIED` document in MongoDB `payment_audit_trail` BEFORE returning the error. Writing the denial audit before throwing ensures downstream analytics always see denied events even when the call chain aborts early.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | COMPLETED TicketSale for Event with eventDate = today (≤ 24h).              | 1) `_EtM2S5.bookWithEvent(this, 1L, 1L, "COMPLETED", dateNear)` where dateNear=today.<br>2) `_EtM2S5.sale(this, bid, 1L, 100.0, "COMPLETED", dateNear, null)`.<br>3) Snapshot count of `payment_audit_trail` docs with action="REFUND_DENIED".<br>4) POST `/api/sales/{sid}/refund-window-policy`.<br>5) Assert HTTP 400.<br>6) Re-count `payment_audit_trail` action="REFUND_DENIED"; assert grew by ≥ 1.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | • HTTP 400 (window expired)<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Time-based dispatch missing — past-event TicketSales still trigger refunds.<br>• payment_audit_trail REFUND_DENIED count grew<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Denial path skips the Observer fire before throwing — analytics never see denial events.                                                                                                                                                     |
| 385 | TC385 — DP-1 Strategy: refund service has no if-else on event timing       | `public`, `patterns`, `strategy`                | (source scan)                                                                                    | Per Section 3.2 step (g), the S5-F12 service method body must NOT contain inline branching on hours-until-event (`if (hours > 48)`, `ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(...)`, ternary on event timing, or `switch` on a time window). That decision belongs in the selector. A service that still branches is a façade Strategy: pattern shape without pattern intent — removing or swapping strategies has no effect on the service's behavior.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | None.                                                                       | 1) Locate the S5-F12 service method handling `/api/sales/{id}/refund-window-policy`.<br>2) Source-scan the method body for `if (hours`, `ChronoUnit.HOURS`, `hoursUntilEvent`, ternary `>= 48`, or `switch` on a time window.<br>3) Assert zero matches inside the service method body.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | • No time-window branching in service method<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Strategy classes exist but service still owns the dispatch — changing strategy implementations in isolation has no effect on behavior.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| 386 | TC386 — DP-2 Observer: EntityObserver interface                            | `public`, `patterns`, `observer`                | (reflection — no HTTP)                                                                           | Per Section 3.3, the GoF Observer requires an `EntityObserver` interface with method `onEvent(String eventType, Object payload)`. Every concrete observer must implement this contract so subjects can register observers polymorphically without knowing their concrete type. Asserting the interface shape before any HTTP integration ensures the basic pattern scaffolding is in place.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | None.                                                                       | 1) Reflection-load `EntityObserver`.<br>2) Assert it is an interface.<br>3) Assert it declares method `onEvent(String, Object)` (or equivalent two-argument signature).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | • EntityObserver is an interface<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Observer modeled as a concrete class — multiple observer kinds cannot coexist on one subject.<br>• onEvent(String, Object) declared<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Method named differently or wrong arity — register/notify cannot compile against a uniform handler signature.                                                                                                                    |
| 387 | TC387 — DP-2 Observer: MongoEventLogger implements EntityObserver          | `public`, `patterns`, `observer`                | (reflection — no HTTP)                                                                           | Per Section 3.3, `MongoEventLogger` is the concrete observer that persists events into MongoDB. It must implement `EntityObserver` so subjects treat it polymorphically — replacing it with a stub or unregistering it must not require source changes elsewhere. A broken implementation hierarchy here means every service that wires the logger is wiring a concrete class, defeating the pattern.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | TC386 passes.                                                               | 1) Reflection-load `MongoEventLogger`.<br>2) Assert `EntityObserver.class.isAssignableFrom(MongoEventLogger.class)`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | • MongoEventLogger implements EntityObserver<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Logger built without inheriting from the interface — forces services to know they're talking to MongoEventLogger specifically.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 388 | TC388 — DP-2 Observer: no @EventListener writes to MongoDB (Spring vs GoF) | `public`, `patterns`, `observer`               | (static analysis)                                                                                | Per Section 3.3, M2 mandates classical GoF Observer (explicit interface + register/notify methods). Spring's `@EventListener` is forbidden on the MongoDB-write path because using it would skip the learning objective of building the pattern manually. The spec calls this out explicitly: no method annotated with `@EventListener` may write to MongoDB. This scan covers all 5 services.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | None.                                                                       | 1) Source/class-file scan all 5 services.<br>2) Find every method annotated with `@EventListener`.<br>3) For each, assert no MongoDB write (no `mongoTemplate`, no Spring Data Mongo `Repository.save`/`insert`).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | • Zero @EventListener methods write to MongoDB<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Team implemented event logging via Spring's built-in publisher and shipped a no-op classical Observer just to satisfy reflection — defeats the M2 learning objective.                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| 389 | TC389 — DP-2 Observer: register triggers REGISTERED in auth_events         | `public`, `patterns`, `observer`                | `POST /api/auth/register`                                                                        | Per Section 3.3 step (d), registration must propagate through the observer chain and produce exactly one `REGISTERED` document in `auth_events` with the new user's id. This is the end-to-end check that the chain is wired from the controller through the subject through the MongoEventLogger. Any break in the chain — missing wiring, wrong action string, or wrong userId — fails here.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | MongoDB reachable.                                                          | 1) POST `/api/auth/register` with a fresh nonce-based email.<br>2) Capture the resulting user id.<br>3) Query `auth_events` filtered by userId AND action="REGISTERED".<br>4) Assert exactly 1 document with a recent timestamp.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | • 1 REGISTERED document with matching userId<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Auth controller never invokes notifyObservers (most common retrofit miss); or fires it but with the wrong userId/action.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 390 | TC390 — DP-2 Observer: login triggers LOGGED_IN                            | `public`, `patterns`, `observer`                | `POST /api/auth/login`                                                                           | Per Section 3.3 step (e), login produces a `LOGGED_IN` document on every successful authentication. Sister test to TC389 — proves both auth controllers fire the chain, not just register. A team that only wired register would pass TC389 but fail here.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Registered user.                                                            | 1) Snapshot count of `auth_events` for that userId.<br>2) Login with valid credentials.<br>3) Re-snapshot count.<br>4) Assert grew by ≥ 1 with action=LOGGED_IN.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | • New LOGGED_IN doc per login<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Login bypasses observer chain — only register fires notifications, leaving login silent in the audit trail.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| 391 | TC391 — DP-2 Observer: M1 retrofit (S1-F2) emits event                     | `public`, `patterns`, `observer`                | `PUT /api/users/{id}/preferences` (M1 S1-F2)                                                    | Per Section 3.3 step (f) and Section 4.5, every M1 write endpoint must be retrofitted to fire the observer chain. PUT preferences (M1 S1-F2) is the canonical example: invoking this M1 endpoint must produce a MongoDB event, proving the retrofit isn't restricted to M2 features. The collection is `auth_events` and the action is `USER_UPDATED` or theme-equivalent.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Registered user with valid token.                                           | 1) Snapshot `auth_events` count for the user.<br>2) PUT `/api/users/{id}/preferences` with body `{"language":"fr"}`.<br>3) Re-snapshot.<br>4) Assert count grew by ≥ 1 (action `USER_UPDATED` or equivalent).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | • M1 write produces a MongoDB event<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** M2 features fire observers but M1 retrofits skipped — audit trail incomplete on legacy paths.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| 392 | TC392 — DP-2 Observer: unregister stops events (proves chain path)         | `public`, `patterns`, `observer`                | (unit test)                                                                                      | Per Section 3.3 step (g), removing all observers from the subject must stop MongoDB writes. This is the reverse-corner check: it proves the logging path goes through the explicit observer chain rather than via a hidden direct Mongo call inside the service. Hand-rolled `Document` writes with slightly different field names would fail deserialization into AuthEvent later.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Mongo reachable.                                                            | 1) POST `/api/auth/register` with fresh email.<br>2) Read the latest `auth_events` document.<br>3) Assert it has `action="REGISTERED"`, a `userId`, and a `timestamp`.<br>4) Compare structure (field names, types) against a factory-built reference: `EventFactory.createEvent(AUTH, params)`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | • Persisted doc has AuthEvent shape<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Service writes a hand-rolled `Document` directly (bypasses factory), with subtly different field names that won't deserialize into AuthEvent.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| 419 | TC419 — DP-6 Factory: no `new XEvent(...)` in services                     | `public`, `patterns`, `factory`                 | (source scan)                                                                                    | Per Section 3.7 step (8), all event construction must go through the factory. Direct `new AuthEvent(...)` or `new TicketEvent(...)` calls in service code defeat the factory's centralization — changes in factory logic (e.g., adding tracing or default fields) won't reach those services.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | None.                                                                       | 1) Source-scan all 5 services (excluding the factory's own implementation).<br>2) Search for `new AuthEvent(`, `new EventActivityEvent(`, `new BookingEvent(`, `new TicketEvent(`, `new PaymentAuditEvent(`.<br>3) Assert zero matches in service classes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | • No direct event constructors in service classes<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Service bypassed the factory in one or more code paths — factory centralization has no effect on those paths.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| 420 | TC420 — DP-7 Adapter: per-service NoSQL adapter classes                    | `public`, `patterns`, `adapter`                 | (reflection — no HTTP)                                                                           | Per Section 3.8, each service must have one adapter per NoSQL source it uses: `MongoDocumentAdapter` in all 5 services (MongoDB); `ElasticsearchHitAdapter` in catalog-service (S2 events index); `Neo4jRecordAdapter` in order/booking-service (S3 User-ATTENDED→Event graph); `CassandraRowAdapter` in ticket-service (S4 ticket_scan_events timeseries).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | None.                                                                       | 1) For each service: reflection-load expected adapter classes per the spec mapping.<br>2) Assert each named class exists in its respective service classpath.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | • All required adapter classes present<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Service does inline raw-NoSQL → DTO conversion in the controller or service — no Adapter encapsulation, logic cannot be tested or swapped in isolation.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| 421 | TC421 — DP-7 Adapter: each adapter has adapt() returning service DTO       | `public`, `patterns`, `adapter`                 | (reflection — no HTTP)                                                                           | Per Section 3.8, each adapter has an `adapt(source) → targetDto` method returning the service's specific domain DTO. There is NO universal "EntityDto" base type — each adapter targets its own DTO. This enforces type safety at each service boundary.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | TC420 passes.                                                               | 1) For each adapter: locate `adapt(...)` method.<br>2) Assert it has a single parameter typed to its NoSQL source (`Document`, `SearchHit`, `Record`, `Row`).<br>3) Assert return type is the service's domain DTO.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | • adapt() signatures correct<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Adapter returns `Map<String,Object>` generically — defeats type safety; or takes generic `Object` parameter — defeats compile-time enforcement.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| 422 | TC422 — DP-7 Adapter: MongoDocumentAdapter.adapt(Document) → DTO           | `public`, `patterns`, `adapter`                 | (unit test)                                                                                      | Per Section 3.8 step (3), passing a mock MongoDB `Document` to `MongoDocumentAdapter.adapt(...)` must yield a populated DTO. Validates the mapping logic in isolation, before running any integration test. Field-name mismatches (camelCase DTO vs snake_case in Mongo) surface here.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | None.                                                                       | 1) Build a mock `org.bson.Document` with sample fields (e.g., `userId=1`, `action="LOGGED_IN"`, `timestamp=...`).<br>2) Call `MongoDocumentAdapter.adapt(doc)`.<br>3) Assert returned DTO has each field populated from the document's keys.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | • DTO fields match Document keys<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Field-name mismatch (camelCase DTO vs snake_case stored in Mongo); or null-handling absent so adapter NPEs on missing optional keys.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 423 | TC423 — DP-7 Adapter: ElasticsearchHitAdapter (catalog-service)            | `public`, `patterns`, `adapter`                 | (unit test)                                                                                      | Per Section 3.8 step (4), the catalog-service `ElasticsearchHitAdapter.adapt(SearchHit)` converts an Elasticsearch search hit into the Event DTO with fields like `name`, `specialty`, and `rating` populated from the hit's source map. This covers the `events` search index used by S2-F1 Elasticsearch full-text search.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | None.                                                                       | 1) Build a mock `SearchHit` with sourceAsMap = `{name:"Dr. Smith", specialty:"Dentist", rating:4.5}`.<br>2) Call `ElasticsearchHitAdapter.adapt(hit)`.<br>3) Assert DTO has name="Dr. Smith", specialty="Dentist", rating=4.5.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | • Event DTO populated from SearchHit<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Adapter calls `hit.getId()` but ignores `hit.getSourceAsMap()` — DTO has id but no business fields; clients see empty event cards in search results.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 424 | TC424 — DP-7 Adapter: ObjectArrayDtoAdapter for S1-F3                      | `public`, `patterns`, `adapter`                 | (reflection + integration)                                                                       | Per Section 3.8 step (5), S1-F3 explicitly mandates `Object[]` from native SQL (per the M1 spec). The Adapter retrofit requires an `ObjectArrayDtoAdapter` (or similar named class) that converts the `Object[]` rows into `UserBookingSummaryDTO`. Combined check: class exists AND S1-F3 returns the correct DTO via the endpoint.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | User with COMPLETED bookings.                                               | 1) Reflection-scan user-service for an adapter class converting `Object[]` → `UserBookingSummaryDTO`.<br>2) Assert it exists.<br>3) Invoke S1-F3: GET `/api/users/{id}/booking-summary`.<br>4) Assert response is well-formed with `totalBookings`, `completedBookings`, `totalSpent` populated.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | • Adapter class exists<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** S1-F3 still mapped inline in service — `Object[]` → DTO logic embedded in the service method, no adapter encapsulation.<br>• S1-F3 returns correct DTO<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Adapter exists but wires fields incorrectly — wrong totals, null fields, or wrong field positions in the Object[] destructuring.                                                                                        |
| 425 | TC425 — DP-7 Adapter: M1 features using JPQL/DTO projection are exempt     | `public`, `patterns`, `adapter`                 | (source scan)                                                                                    | Per Section 3.8 step (6), only Object[]-using M1 features need an adapter. Features implemented via JPQL constructor expressions (`SELECT new com.x.SomeDTO(...)`) or `@Query` DTO projection are exempt. This TC documents the exemption: the grader does NOT require an adapter for those features, preventing false-positive failures on compliant implementations.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | None.                                                                       | 1) For each M1 F3/F6/F9 service method: source-scan its repository's `@Query` annotation.<br>2) If native SQL with `Object[]` → require adapter (covered by TC424 for S1-F3 specifically).<br>3) If JPQL constructor expression or DTO projection → exempt; assert no failure for missing adapter.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | • Adapter required only for Object[] features<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;- **Bug it catches:** Grader incorrectly fails JPQL-based features — false positive against exempt features that this TC explicitly documents as exempt.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
